previous_picks.rationale: MRCY shows explosive momentum with +49.82% YTD, +22.75% in the last month, a beta of 0.95, and positive news sentiment of 0.138. It is riding strong backlog expectations, and the recent price consolidation after a multi-period gain offers an attractive entry point.

RR.L delivers robust momentum with +59% 1-year return, +17.65% YTD, and +12.76% in the last month, backed by a solid P/E of 20.41 and a favourable analyst buy score of 1.60. The UK defence surge and nuclear propulsion catalyst support continued outperformance.

RTX combines a massive $271bn backlog, a recent $515m SPY-6 radar contract win with plans to double output by 2028, and a +6.81% 1-month move, while trading 14% below its 52-week high with a buy analyst score of 1.87 and positive news sentiment of 0.136.

TransDigm shows renewed momentum with a +12.66% 1-month gain and +5.62% in the last week, supported by a buy analyst score of 1.82 and positive news sentiment of 0.244, despite its high P/E of 41.5 which reflects its pricing power and unique aftermarket positioning.

Leonardo DRS has strong YTD performance of +33.02% with a low beta of 0.12, a compelling P/E of 43.07, and the highest news sentiment in the weapons segment at 0.343, reflecting favourable backlog and multi-year defence spending tailwinds.

Despite China export restrictions and recent volatility, RCAT has surged +24.89% YTD and +33.80% in the last month, with positive news sentiment of 0.176, driven by new Hellcat drone launch and Japan tactical ISR deployment program.

SAIC offers value with a P/E of 11.52 and a +1.89% YTD return, coupled with positive news sentiment of 0.135. The recent +7.42% monthly gain and short interest of 7.7% suggest potential squeeze dynamics amidst solid Q1 results.

HEICO delivers strong near-term momentum with a +15.30% one-month gain, a low beta of 1.035, and a buy analyst score of 1.83, underpinned by consistent demand in aerospace aftermarket and defence components.

AXON shows a reversal with a +7.92% one-month gain despite a -24.86% YTD drawdown, backed by a strong buy score (1.60) and positive news sentiment of 0.087. The high P/E of 169.36 is justified by its dominant position in law enforcement tech and recurring software revenue.

SPCX gains from high IPO demand and index inclusion catalysts (Russell 1000 & MSCI), with +14.94% YTD and +14.94% one-month return, though lockup expirations and volatility warrant caution. The recent pullback from highs may offer a re-entry for patient investors.
